metrics.narrative: Omnia is digitizing an entirely manual, Excel-based procurement process spanning roughly ZAR 16 billion (~USD 1 billion) of spend, with 15 sourcing events and 18 contracts in flight (11 under frequent follow-up) at the time of the 8 Oct 2025 alignment call. Marianne Ventura also flagged a standing operational pain point: spend data currently lags 3 weeks after month-close ('I can't do monthly... we need our data quicker than that,' 30 Jun 2026). These are buyer-quantified, not rep-asserted, figures.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 'SOW discussion and closure call'
champion.narrative: Marianne Ventura (Senior Category Manager: IT & Communications, per Salesforce) has driven the deal across every call from the Oct 2025 executive session through the 30 Jun 2026 closure call, personally resolving redlines line-by-line and stating, 'I really, really expect that we're going to walk away with the signature,' and later, 'we've gotta go in a couple of days, and we have to report we're ready to sign.' Her engagement is current, senior, and unambiguous.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 'SOW discussion and closure call'
competition.narrative: The only named incumbent is Adobe Sign for e-signature, criticized by Ashen Chathury for a 'lack of workflow integration and a 60% adoption rate' (24 Apr 2025) and now formally displaced by Zycus's own Certinal (confirmed 30 Jun 2026). A single unspecified reference to 'considering Zycus among other options' surfaced once, on 24 Apr 2025, and was never repeated across fourteen subsequent months of deepening engagement.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 'SOW discussion and closure call'
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-documented and buyer-voiced: fully manual, Excel-governed sourcing and contracting across 15+ events and 18 contracts; fragmented legacy source systems (SharePoint, Teams, file servers, and multiple ERPs including AX2012 and QAD) forcing Indonesia and the DRC out of the initial rollout; and a 3-week lag on spend data. Marianne Ventura also cited a 2020 cyber incident that left supplier-master data incomplete, compounding the case for a single source of truth.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 'SOW discussion and closure call'
paper_process.narrative: By 30 Jun 2026 the SOW redlines were fully worked through comment-by-comment (country phasing, spend threshold, e-signature vendor) and accepted; the spend threshold was raised from ZAR 900 million to ZAR 1 billion at no commercial uplift, and Certinal was confirmed as the e-signature vendor. Two updated MSA/Order Form documents were committed to follow by email the same day. The signature owner on Zycus's side for the SOW is the AVP Global Delivery (role only, per standard practice); the deal is at the classic SOW choke point immediately ahead of signature.

sources: Avoma call 2025-10-13 'FW: Source to Contract Demo and Business Case'
economic_buyer.narrative: The unnamed Omnia legal director/board member and Jacques de Villiers (Group: Head of Supply Chain, per Salesforce) were named by Marianne Ventura as the two sign-off authorities whose approval unlocks budget: 'I have the legal director in this meeting... his signing off on this is important to me, along with Jacques de Villiers.' Both attended the 13 Oct 2025 executive session held specifically to secure the funding commitment. No direct engagement with either has been documented since that date, roughly nine months before this sweep.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 'SOW discussion and closure call'
decision_process.narrative: The process ran discovery (Apr 2025) through executive funding approval (Oct 2025), design/SOW iteration (Feb-Jun 2026), and into a defined legal sign-off sequence: Omnia completes an internal legal-cover signature in Adobe Sign for its own audit trail, then routes the package to Zycus for countersignature. Marianne Ventura named the sequence explicitly on 30 Jun 2026 and noted a prior slip: 'Delay noted from January to March due to external counsel.'

decision_criteria.narrative: Detailed evaluation criteria were built out through 2026 via sample design-document walkthroughs (implementation approach document + technical design document, process flows, user stories, configuration worksheets) and an explicit requirement that every functionality demonstrated during sales be captured and enforceable in the contract. Omnia's legal reviewer pressed on this directly: 'everything that has been demonstrated is in the statement of work.'

The champion, Marianne Ventura, committed to complete Omnia's internal legal sign-off and route the SOW/MSA/Order Forms for countersignature within days. A separate morning check-in the same day was held but not recorded.

competitive_position.summary: Only one competitor has ever been named across the account's history: the incumbent e-signature tool Adobe Sign, which was displaced by Certinal well before this sweep. No rival source-to-contract vendor has been named at any point.

competitors.name: Adobe Sign
competitors.quote: Ashen Chathury expressed concerns with Adobe Sign, noting a lack of workflow integration and a 60% adoption rate at Omnia.
competitors.how_we_win: Certinal was demonstrated with transaction-based pricing, embedded iContract integration, and was formally confirmed as the e-signature vendor by 30 Jun 2026.

competitors.name: Unnamed alternative S2C vendors (background)
competitors.quote: Omnia is exploring a source-to-contract system and considering Zycus among other options.
competitors.how_we_win: No named rival resurfaced across the following fourteen-plus months of demos, design, and SOW negotiation leading to a signature-ready contract.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Real, deeply-documented buyer engagement puts this deal on the doorstep of a signed SOW and MSA, even though the opportunity record itself was created only three days ago with no logged activity, no logged contact roles, and a close date that cannot be reconciled with the evidence.

deal_risk.text: Sanctions/compliance-screening integration (World-Check One vs. DNB/Refinitiv/iOxcio) remains unresolved and deferred to the design phase, an open dependency ahead of go-live.

Whether an external implementation partner is needed alongside the vendor has been raised twice (Oct 2025 and 30 Jun 2026) without a final decision, creating resourcing ambiguity heading into kickoff.

win_position.text: SOW fully redlined and scope locked — the 30 Jun 2026 closure call resolved every open comment (country phasing, spend threshold, e-sign vendor) and the champion confirmed she is routing the package for internal legal sign-off within days.

Economic buyer identified with direct engagement — an Omnia director/board-level legal sponsor and the Group Head of Supply Chain both attended the 13 Oct 2025 executive session held specifically to secure the funding commitment.

No confirmed executive contact in roughly nine months — the only documented direct touch with the economic buyer was the 13 Oct 2025 funding presentation; nothing since then verifies the sponsor is still actively behind the deal.

The recorded close date (2031-07-01) and the absence of any logged Salesforce activity on this opportunity make the record itself unreliable as a source of truth, masking how close this deal actually is to signature.

deal_momentum.text: Two calls in the final week of June 2026 (23 Jun integration/SOW review, 30 Jun closure call) show active, high-frequency back-and-forth resolving the last redlines and locking commercial terms.
No verified touch has occurred in the nine days since the 30 Jun 2026 closure call, and the champion's committed 'couple of days' turnaround for legal sign-off has not been confirmed.

customer_commitment.text: The champion personally drove redline resolution line-by-line on the 30 Jun 2026 call and stated she was already preparing to report internally that Omnia is 'ready to sign.'
Omnia raised its own governed spend threshold to ZAR 1 billion and accepted the change at no additional cost, signaling continued institutional buy-in rather than scope retrenchment.
